قول القديس مر اسحق علي سيرة السكوت (f. 144r)
names_tesim: Isaac, Bishop of Nineveh, active 7th century
exerpts_tesim: And surround those at His side with His salvation, to Whom belongs the glory, honor, and worship with His Father who has no beginning and the ever and Life-giving Holy Spirit, now, always, and to the end of the ages. Amen and amen
As it is said, “Take pleasure in me and my children.” Glory be to Him with the Holy Spirit who judges our lives and has authority over them into eternity. Amen
During and in all the days of your lives, respond to God and be reasonable with thanks always
I believe in one God, the Almighty, eternal, who has no beginning in His entire being…
I have striven to take hold of this word, as well, too, of this laughter and this walk
In a proverbial sense of removing darkness from rational minds and polishing the rust from [the door]...
It is not necessary to punish one of us whose likeness is merely as the vice of sinners…
Love silence eagerly, my brother, for you will find life in it for yourself
O beloved brother who yearns with the fervor of love to be with God and without sin
So strive to profit first by your prayers and your methods of the harvest from now and all time and to the age of ages. Amen
We give praise for your humility, and the angels rejoice in heaven for the return of the sinner. Amen
احتال ان تقتني هده الكلمه كذلك ايضا وهده الضحك والمشي
اومن بالاه واحد ضابط كُلّ ازليّ لا مبدا له بالكليّه
ايها الاخ الحبيب الدى قد اشتاق بحرارة المحبّه ان يكون لله ويكون بلا خطيه
حبّ السكوت يا خي بحرص لانّ فيه تجد نفسك الحياه
حين وفي كل ايام حياتك رد الى الله عقلك بشكر دايم
فاجتهد في ان تربح اوليك بصلاتك وبطريقتك الحصده من الان وكل اوان والى دهر الداهرين امين
في معانى تزيل الظلام عن العقول وتجلوا الصدا عن الباب
كالمقول اسرر لي ولبني بيتي السبح الذي بروح قدسه حكّم اميتّنا بها ولى الي الدهر امين
لساياي المواهب والخلاص بدل كل سُبح وشكر في زمان الواجب الذي له المجد وعلينا رحمته الى دهر الداهرين امين
ليس يوجب العقوبه علي احدنا مشابهته رذيلة الخطاه فقط
واحطي بخلاصه بحوده الدي له يليق المجد والاكرام والسجود مع ابيه الدى لا مبدا له وروحه الكلى القدس والمحيي الان ودايما والى اباد الدهور امين وامين
وتقدم نحن السُبح لاذليتك وتفرح في السموات الملايكه علي رجوع الخاطى امين
…to seek these gifts and salvation, instead of every praise and thanks at the necessary time, to whom be the glory and upon us his mercy to the age of ages. Amen

And surround those at His side with His salvation, to Whom belongs the glory, honor, and worship with His Father who has no beginning and the ever and Life-giving Holy Spirit, now, always, and to the end of the ages. Amen and amen
Another section in the movement of the body, ff. 100v - 102v
As it is said, “Take pleasure in me and my children.” Glory be to Him with the Holy Spirit who judges our lives and has authority over them into eternity. Amen
Creed from the words of ʿAbdallāh b. al-Faḍl b. ʿAbdallāh
During and in all the days of your lives, respond to God and be reasonable with thanks always
First section of Mar Isaac, ff. 003r - 014v
I believe in one God, the Almighty, eternal, who has no beginning in His entire being…
I have striven to take hold of this word, as well, too, of this laughter and this walk
In a proverbial sense of removing darkness from rational minds and polishing the rust from [the door]...
It is not necessary to punish one of us whose likeness is merely as the vice of sinners…
Love silence eagerly, my brother, for you will find life in it for yourself
O beloved brother who yearns with the fervor of love to be with God and without sin
Preface and panegyric by ʿAbdallāh ibn al-Faḍl, ff. 001v - 003r
Profitable counsels of John Chrysostom, ff. 159r - 182r
So strive to profit first by your prayers and your methods of the harvest from now and all time and to the age of ages. Amen
Spiritual teaching of the holy fathers, ff. 184r - 188r
The text is a translation of Isaac by ʿAbdallāh ibn al-Faḍl.
The text is divided into several rubricized subsections
Thirty-five Chapters on the Monastic Life by Isaac the Syrian
Views of three saints: Gregory the Theologian, Basil the Great, and John Chrysostom
We give praise for your humility, and the angels rejoice in heaven for the return of the sinner. Amen
